name: Policy and Procedures
description: Develop, document, and disseminate access control policy and procedures that address purpose, scope, roles, responsibilities, management commitment, coordination, and compliance.
guidance: Organizations should document access control policies, review them annually, and ensure all personnel are trained. Procedures should cover account management, access enforcement, and separation of duties.

name: Account Management
description: Manage information system accounts including establishing, activating, modifying, reviewing, disabling, and removing accounts.
guidance: Implement automated tools to support account management. Review accounts regularly (at least annually). Disable inactive accounts after 90 days. Remove accounts for terminated users within 24 hours.

name: Access Enforcement
description: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.
guidance: Implement role-based access control (RBAC) or attribute-based access control (ABAC). Apply the principle of least privilege. Restrict access to sensitive data and system resources.

name: Remote Access
description: Establish and document usage restrictions, configuration/connection requirements, and implementation guidance for each type of remote access allowed.
guidance: Use VPN with MFA for remote access. Encrypt all remote sessions. Monitor remote access sessions. Disconnect inactive remote sessions after 15 minutes.

name: Event Logging
description: Identify the types of events that the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function.
guidance: Log authentication attempts (success and failure), privilege escalation, account management, system startup/shutdown, configuration changes, and access to sensitive data.

name: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
description: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity, and report findings.
guidance: Review audit logs at minimum weekly. Use SIEM for automated analysis. Establish baselines for normal behavior and alert on anomalies.

name: Configuration Settings
description: Establish and document configuration settings for IT products employed within the information system.
guidance: Use CIS Benchmarks or DISA STIGs as configuration baselines. Implement configuration management tools (Ansible, Chef, Puppet). Monitor for configuration drift.

name: Least Functionality
description: Configure the system to provide only essential capabilities. Prohibit or restrict the use of functions, ports, protocols, and services not required.
guidance: Disable unused services and ports. Remove unnecessary software. Implement application whitelisting. Regularly review enabled services.

name: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
description: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and associate that unique identification with processes acting on behalf of those users.
guidance: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all accounts, especially privileged accounts. Use unique identifiers for each user. Never share accounts.

name: Authenticator Management
description: Manage system authenticators by establishing initial authenticator content, minimum and maximum lifetime restrictions, reuse conditions, and composition requirements.
guidance: Enforce minimum 12-character passwords. Require complexity or passphrase approach. Prevent password reuse for last 24 passwords. Use password managers. Implement MFA.

name: Incident Handling
description: Implement an incident handling capability for security incidents that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, eradication, and recovery.
guidance: Develop and test incident response plans annually. Define roles and responsibilities. Establish communication procedures. Conduct tabletop exercises.

name: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
description: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities potentially affecting the system are identified.
guidance: Perform authenticated vulnerability scans at least quarterly. Remediate critical vulnerabilities within 30 days, high within 60 days. Conduct penetration testing annually.

name: Boundary Protection
description: Monitor and control communications at the external boundary of the system and at key internal boundaries within the system.
guidance: Deploy firewalls at network boundaries. Implement network segmentation. Use DMZ for externally facing systems. Monitor all ingress/egress traffic.

name: Protection of Information at Rest
description: Protect the confidentiality and integrity of information at rest.
guidance: Encrypt sensitive data at rest using AES-256. Protect encryption keys using HSM or key management services. Encrypt full disk on laptops and mobile devices.

name: Flaw Remediation
description: Identify, report, and correct information system flaws. Test software updates before installation. Incorporate flaw remediation into the organizational configuration management process.
guidance: Apply security patches within 30 days of release for critical/high vulnerabilities. Track patch status with vulnerability management tools. Test patches before production deployment.

name: Malicious Code Protection
description: Implement malicious code protection mechanisms at system entry and exit points and at workstations, servers, or mobile computing devices on the network.
guidance: Deploy EDR/AV on all endpoints. Update signatures frequently. Scan email attachments and downloads. Configure automatic scanning.
